domains.name: Applications
domains.description: Web and business applications that hold your most sensitive logic and data.
challenges: Insecure coding patterns shipped to production
No continuous security testing in the SDLC
domains.businessRisk: A single application flaw can expose customer data, break compliance posture, or take core revenue systems offline.

domains.name: Cloud
domains.description: Cloud infrastructure and platform services running production workloads.
challenges: Misconfigured storage, networking, and IAM policies
Identity sprawl across multiple cloud accounts
domains.businessRisk: Cloud misconfiguration remains one of the leading causes of large-scale data exposure incidents.

domains.name: Identity
domains.description: Identity and access services governing who can reach what, and how.
challenges: Excess standing privilege across users and services
Orphaned accounts and stale access grants
Fragmented identity across SaaS and on-prem
domains.businessRisk: Identity compromise is the most common entry point in modern breaches — it bypasses most perimeter controls outright.

domains.name: Network
domains.description: The connective layer linking users, services, sites, and the internet.
domains.businessRisk: Without segmentation, a single compromised endpoint can become an open path to your entire environment.

domains.name: Endpoints
domains.description: Laptops, servers, and devices where users and workloads actually run.
challenges: Limited detection coverage on managed devices
domains.businessRisk: Endpoints are the most common initial foothold for ransomware and commodity malware.

domains.name: Data
domains.description: Structured and unstructured data — the asset every other control exists to protect.
challenges: Limited visibility into where sensitive data lives
No consistent encryption or retention policy
domains.businessRisk: Data exposure is usually the headline outcome of a breach — and the hardest to fully remediate after the fact.

domains.name: APIs
domains.description: The interfaces connecting applications, partners, and third-party integrations.
challenges: Excessive data exposure in API responses
No rate limiting or abuse detection
domains.businessRisk: APIs are now a primary attack surface — most breaches in modern stacks touch an API at some stage.

domains.name: Remote Access
domains.description: The pathways remote and hybrid workforces use to reach internal systems.
challenges: Legacy VPN architectures with broad network trust
domains.businessRisk: Remote access is the front door for a distributed workforce — and a frequent target for credential-based attacks.
